title: Nav rollout dispatch 2 — canonical site-nav applied to 61 pages
summary: Applied the canonical site-nav pattern (CSS + HTML + JS) to 61 of the 63 dispatch-2 target pages. 2 files were absent from the repo (board/index.html, essays/index.html — both directories exist but have no index.html stub; not convertible). 0 manual interventions required; the Python script handled all structural variance cleanly. /graph/index.html and /graph/mcp.html were deliberately excluded and remain untouched (reserved for O-1 Sprint 2). The 4 anchor pages from commit cd0b03a (/, /briefings/, /briefings/015.html, /product/) were not in the target list and were not modified.
o1_status: Advances O-1. Every page in this dispatch now carries the AAA-compliant nav landmark: role=navigation aria-label='Site', aria-expanded disclosure button, aria-controls, ESC handler, focus-return-to-trigger, 44px minimum targets, focus-visible rings, prefers-reduced-motion respected, mobile overlay with role=dialog-equivalent (position:fixed, body overflow:hidden). The sn-mobile overlay uses aria-label='Site menu'; the close button has aria-label='Close site menu'. Full keyboard contract from the canonical spec is present on all 61 files. The open keyboard-nav critical (O-1 Sprint 2, affecting /graph/) remains open and is scoped to the next sprint.
approach: Python script: (1) reads each file, (2) skips if class=site-nav already present, (3) inserts CSS block before </head>, (4) inserts HTML nav+mobile-overlay after skip-link if present else after <body>, (5) inserts JS block before </body>. All 63 target files are one level deep — hrefs use '../' prefix throughout. CSS block includes scoped token fallbacks on .site-nav for pages where :root vars might be missing.
deprecations: None introduced in this dispatch. See /design/navigation.html §7 for deprecations from dispatch 1 (anchor pages).
